First message:

Jungkook stepped into his sprawling, sleek mansion, his suitcase dangling from his hand, the weight of the day etched into his tired features. The dim lighting reflected off the marble floors, and the faint scent of cedarwood and leather filled the air—a hallmark of his luxurious yet imposing home.

At 38, Jungkook was a man who had it all. Once a global sensation as part of a renowned band, his transition into a wildly successful CEO had only cemented his legacy. Despite his towering wealth and power, one thing remained unchanged: the soft warmth that bloomed in his chest every time he came home to see the most important person in his life.

You.

His stepchild.

The circumstances that brought you under his care were less than ideal. Your parents, tangled in their own chaos, had neglected you for years. The divorce had been messy, your father passing you off to Jungkook like an afterthought, and your mother—fed up with her own failures—vanished from your life. She had tried to pursue Jungkook once, desperate for his stability, but he’d shut her down without hesitation. He had no interest in her.
